news.headline: Caribou CEO touts off-the-shelf CAR-T cost advantage at H.C. Wainwright Cell Therapy Conference
news.summary: President and CEO Rachel Haurwitz held a fireside chat at the H.C. Wainwright 4th Annual Cell Therapy Virtual Conference, arguing that vispa-cel could launch with cost of goods sold roughly 96% lower than autologous CAR-T therapies. She pointed to the vispa-cel and CB-011 data presented at EHA in June as evidence the off-the-shelf approach can match autologous CAR-T efficacy.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences shareholders elect directors, reject officer-exculpation charter change at annual meeting
news.summary: At its virtual annual meeting, Caribou shareholders elected three Class II directors (Andrew Guggenhime, David Johnson, and Nancy Whiting) and ratified Deloitte & Touche as auditor for fiscal 2026. Investors rejected a proposed charter amendment that would have expanded exculpation protections for company officers under Delaware law.

news.headline: Caribou boosts case that off-the-shelf CAR-T matches autologous drugs ahead of Phase 3
news.summary: Ahead of its EHA 2026 oral presentation, Caribou disclosed that vispa-cel achieved a 17.1-month median progression-free survival in second-line lymphoma patients, bolstering the case that its allogeneic therapy can rival autologous CAR-T. The planned pivotal ANTLER-3 Phase 3 trial will target patients who are ineligible for autologous CAR-T or cannot access it, such as those living too far from treatment centers.

news.headline: Caribou reports dose-escalation durability data for CB-011 at EHA 2026
news.summary: A single dose of CB-011 produced deep, durable responses in heavily pretreated, BCMA-naive relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients, with an 83% complete response rate, 91% MRD negativity, and 50% of patients still in complete response at 15 months. Data were presented in an oral session at the EHA 2026 Annual Meeting in Stockholm.

news.headline: Caribou reports long-term vispa-cel (CB-010) data in second-line large B-cell lymphoma at EHA 2026
news.summary: Updated ANTLER Phase 1 data showed vispa-cel (formerly CB-010) achieved an 82% overall response rate, 67% complete response rate, and 17.1-month median progression-free survival in an optimized 27-patient second-line LBCL subgroup, durability Caribou says is on par with autologous CAR-T therapies. The release also disclosed two vispa-cel-related or possibly related patient deaths in the trial, underscoring that safety remains a watch item alongside the efficacy data.

news.headline: Caribou reports Q1 2026 results: FDA alignment on pivotal ANTLER-3 trial, RMAT-backed CB-011 momentum
news.summary: Caribou disclosed that it reached alignment with the FDA on the design of the pivotal ANTLER-3 Phase 3 trial for vispa-cel and reported cash and securities of $118.6 million as of March 31, 2026, guided to fund operations into the second half of 2027. The update followed CB-011's FDA RMAT designation, granted March 31, 2026, based on a 92% ORR and 75% complete-response rate in an initial BCMA-naive cohort.

catalysts.label: CaMMouflage Phase 1 dose-expansion data for CB-011
catalysts.detail: Initial dose-expansion data from the CaMMouflage Phase 1 trial of CB-011 in BCMA-naive and BCMA-exposed relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients is expected in the second half of 2026, building on the dose-escalation durability data presented at EHA in June. CB-011 also carries FDA Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation, granted March 31, 2026.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings and business update
catalysts.detail: Caribou is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and provide a pipeline update in early-to-mid August 2026 per consensus earnings-calendar estimates, likely including further detail on ANTLER-3 trial start-up progress and updated cash-runway guidance; as of July 22 the company has not formally confirmed the exact date via press release.

catalysts.label: Pivotal ANTLER-3 Phase 3 trial start-up for vispa-cel
catalysts.detail: Following FDA alignment reached in early May 2026 on trial design (a randomized, controlled study in CD19-naive, transplant-ineligible 2L LBCL patients who cannot access or are not candidates for autologous CAR-T), Caribou is funding start-up activities for the pivotal ANTLER-3 trial, with cash runway guided into 2H 2027; a formal trial initiation/first-patient-dosed date has not been disclosed.

catalysts.label: Additional CB-010/CB-011 data at a major hematology conference (e.g., ASH)
catalysts.detail: Caribou has a pattern of presenting incremental follow-up data on vispa-cel and CB-011 at major medical conferences following EHA in June 2026; further updates are plausible at the ASH Annual Meeting in December 2026, though not yet formally announced.

pulse: Caribou remains in a quiet stretch heading into Q2 2026 earnings, expected in early-to-mid August but still not formally confirmed by the company, with the EHA 2026 data from June (vispa-cel durability in lymphoma, deep and durable CB-011 responses in myeloma) still the dominant narrative alongside management's H.C. Wainwright pitch that off-the-shelf CAR-T can launch at roughly 96% lower cost of goods than autologous therapies. Shareholders reaffirmed the board at the June 17 annual meeting while rejecting a proposed expansion of officer liability protections, and the pivotal ANTLER-3 trial start-up remains the next concrete milestone to watch after FDA trial-design alignment in May. The stock trades in the roughly $1.75-$2.20 range (market cap near $190 million, close to its 52-week low) even though sell-side coverage stays uniformly Strong Buy with consensus price targets clustered well above the current share price (roughly $8-$12), reflecting a persistent gap between analyst optimism about the allogeneic CAR-T platform and market skepticism given the company's modest cash position and looming financing needs. Watch items into year-end include CB-011 dose-expansion data, any update on ANTLER-3 start-up timing, and cash-runway guidance (last reported at $118.6M, into 2H 2027) at the next earnings report.
